title: Internal Description: The State-Space Description
summary: This page introduces the state-space description as an internal description of linear systems, defining state variables as key variables from which every signal in the system can be expressed as a linear combination. An RLC circuit example is used to illustrate how capacitor voltages and inductor currents serve as state variables, demonstrating that knowing their values along with the input allows determination of all other circuit signals. The page also briefly discusses uncontrollable and unobservable systems as undesirable in practice.

figures.caption: Structures of uncontrollable and unobservable systems.
figures.description: The figure shows two block diagrams inside dashed system boundaries, labeled (a) and (b), with signal arrows running from input x(t) on the left to output y(t) on the right. In (a), subsystems S1 and S2 both feed a summing junction Σ before y(t), illustrating an uncontrollable structure where one internal mode is not directly influenced by the input path. In (b), the input branches to subsystems S1 and S2, while y(t) is taken from the S1 path only, illustrating an unobservable structure where an internal subsystem affects state evolution but not the measured output. Students should notice how connectivity of x(t), y(t), S1, S2, and the summing node determines controllability and observability.
